sourceMovie.title: Sisters
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Sisters for the same reason it broke through in 2015: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Jason Moore keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Ratatouille
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Sisters clicked for you, Ratatouille (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Sisters?
faq.answer: Try Ratatouille, Little Miss Sunshine, The Edge of Seventeen—each shares DNA with Sisters while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Sisters?
faq.answer: Sisters (2015) is best described as Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Sisters worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
